title: toxicodendron discolor
intro: Toxicodendron discolor, also known as poison sumac, is a plant species native to the southeastern United States and parts of Asia, belonging to the Anacardiaceae family. The plant contains urushiol, a toxic resin that causes severe skin irritation and allergic reactions in humans. In traditional medicine, poison sumac has been used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be used to treat various health conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. Herbal preparations of poison sumac can be made into teas, ointments, or salves, but due to its toxicity, it's essential to use it under the guidance of a medical professional and follow proper handling and preparation procedures.
